name: Reuters
description: International wire service. Consistently factual, neutral political framing, low sensationalism. Widely used as a reference baseline.

name: The Hindu
description: Chennai-based broadsheet. High factual standards, consistent left-liberal editorial lean, low sensationalism.

name: Times of India
description: India's largest English daily by circulation. Centrist-right ownership, frequently criticised for clickbait headlines and advertorial content.

name: NDTV
description: Delhi-based TV news channel. Generally opposition-leaning framing, moderate sensationalism in prime-time coverage.

name: Republic World
description: Indian news channel founded by Arnab Goswami. Strong pro-government lean, high sensationalism, aggressive debate format.

name: The Print
description: Delhi-based digital outlet. Generally factual with a liberal-centrist lean. Known for policy analysis and investigative pieces.

name: The Wire
description: Independent digital outlet. Strong left-liberal editorial stance, focus on civil liberties and government accountability.

name: Hindustan Times
description: Delhi-based English daily. Mild centre-right lean, moderate sensationalism, broad national coverage.

name: Indian Express
description: Mumbai-based broadsheet. Generally factual, mild liberal lean, strong investigative journalism tradition.

name: ANI
description: Asian News International — India's largest wire service. Frequently cited as having pro-government sourcing bias, widely syndicated.
